blockers.title: Source-to-target schema, data, auth, storage, and configuration parity is unproven

verification.method: Run the strict cutover comparator against the frozen source and final target, then complete the Dashboard configuration parity attestation.
successCriteria: Comparator reports zero P0 and P1 findings for gadpooereceldfpfxsod.
Auth user count, storage byte copy, migrations, RLS, grants, functions, cron, Realtime, and tenant financial aggregates reconcile.
Every approved database migration is present in one declared canonical migration sequence; no required migration remains only in an ungoverned parallel directory.
Manual configuration parity confirms extensions, Edge Functions, secrets, SMTP, CAPTCHA, network controls, and backups.
evidenceRequirements: Redacted strict comparator result with target reference and zero findings.
Redacted storage byte verification and configuration parity attestation hashes.

blockers.title: Public join and booking paths lack production proof of abuse resistance

verification.method: Deploy the public gateway controls and test anonymous request throttling, validation, replay handling, and denied direct RPC access on the final target.
successCriteria: Public write paths use a controlled server-side gateway with strict origin rules and no PII in logs.
Rate limits are keyed by a privacy-preserving identifier and reject bursts without creating duplicate customers, bookings, or consents.
Direct anonymous PostgREST and RPC paths cannot bypass gateway validation.
evidenceRequirements: Redacted anonymous smoke and negative-test result.
Redacted grant and function exposure inventory after deployment.

blockers.title: Customer booking lookup and change requests are not proven to require a scoped token

verification.method: Exercise booking management using a single-use or expiring opaque token and run token-missing, token-replay, cross-booking, and phone-only negative tests.
successCriteria: A phone number alone cannot list or modify appointments.
Only a hashed management token is stored, and a token is limited to its booking and allowed action window.
Legacy phone-only public RPC grants are revoked or are inaccessible from anonymous traffic.
evidenceRequirements: Redacted booking-management positive and negative test report.
Redacted function-grant inventory proving legacy exposure is absent.

blockers.title: Final target RLS, function grants, and tenant isolation have not been adversarially verified

verification.method: Run a rolled-back multi-tenant adversarial test as owner, staff, anonymous, and unaffiliated principals after all target migrations and functions are deployed.
successCriteria: Cross-tenant reads, writes, function calls, and direct financial access fail closed.
Only explicit public gateway functions have anonymous execute rights.
Security-definer functions have safe search paths and enforce tenant ownership.
evidenceRequirements: Redacted RLS adversarial test summary with real-principal controls.
Redacted function grants and policy inventory.

blockers.title: Sale reversal, refund, and store-credit tender integrity are not production-verified

verification.method: Apply v20, then execute rolled-back full refund, partial-refund rejection, over-refund, retry, store-credit tender, and cross-tenant test cases.
successCriteria: Corrections are append-only, atomic, idempotent, role-gated, and auditable.
Revenue, receivables, payments, cash, points, credit, referral, inventory, and commission effects reconcile after each supported correction.
Unsupported partial or value-redemption cases are rejected explicitly and are absent from the user interface.
evidenceRequirements: Redacted transaction rollback test report and ledger reconciliation summary.
Redacted role and direct-insert denial report.

blockers.title: Reporting pagination, exports, and Singapore time boundaries are not verified at production scale

verification.method: Run reporting RPC and export tests against representative staging and production-safe fixture volumes, including a Singapore month boundary and records beyond the API default page size.
successCriteria: No dashboard or export path silently truncates data at default API limits.
Report totals agree with ledger and sale snapshots across SGT day and month boundaries.
Long-running report queries meet the agreed operational limit without exposing another tenant's data.
evidenceRequirements: Redacted pagination and reconciliation report.
Redacted performance sample with query identifiers and durations only.

blockers.title: PDPA public notices, consent operations, and data-subject handling are not production-proven

verification.method: Publish and smoke-test the privacy, terms, and data-request pages, then rehearse access, correction, withdrawal, retention, and incident escalation procedures with synthetic data only.
successCriteria: Public notices state the relevant purposes, marketing consent, processors, Singapore hosting, retention, security measures, and contact route without unsupported compliance claims.
Consent is opt-in and an operator can fulfill access, correction, withdrawal, and deletion decisions using the documented procedure.
The incident process assigns an owner, decision log, containment, and notification assessment.
evidenceRequirements: Redacted policy-page smoke report with release commit.
Redacted synthetic-data operations rehearsal record.

blockers.title: Password reset, email confirmation, SMTP, and anti-automation settings are not production-verified

verification.method: Validate final Supabase Auth settings and run synthetic sign-up, confirmation, reset, expiry, redirect-allowlist, and brute-force resistance tests through the deployed URL.
successCriteria: Email confirmation, leaked-password protection, password policy, CAPTCHA or equivalent control, and intended redirect URLs are enabled.
A production SMTP provider delivers confirmation and reset messages with no development sender or test recipient dependency.
Authentication failures are rate-limited and observable without logging credentials or tokens.
evidenceRequirements: Redacted Auth configuration checklist and synthetic mailbox test results.
Redacted login, confirmation, and recovery smoke report.

blockers.title: Customer and staff notification behavior is not operationally defined and proven

verification.method: Configure the chosen notification provider or explicitly disable outbound promises, then test booking, change, reminder, failure, retry, unsubscribe, and staff-alert flows with synthetic recipients.
successCriteria: Every customer-facing promise maps to a delivered, retried, and observable communication or is removed from the product.
Marketing and transactional messages have separate consent and opt-out behavior.
Provider failures cannot duplicate bookings, sales, loyalty credits, or appointment changes.
evidenceRequirements: Redacted provider configuration and synthetic-delivery result.
Redacted retry, opt-out, and failure-handling test report.

blockers.title: Payment collection and SaaS subscription behavior are not truthful and production-proven

verification.method: Test the actual payment and subscription operating model, including webhook verification and failed-payment handling, or verify that collection is manual and no unsupported automation is advertised or exposed.
successCriteria: The product makes no claim that conflicts with its real payment collection, refunds, subscription, tax, or invoicing capability.
Where an online provider is enabled, webhook signatures, idempotency, reconciliation, cancellation, and failure states are tested.
Where collection is manual, the responsible operator, invoice process, and access-control consequence are documented and tested.
evidenceRequirements: Redacted commercial-flow test report or manual-collection operating attestation.
Redacted reconciliation result for a synthetic payment lifecycle.

blockers.title: Backups, PITR, migration rollback, and post-cutover rollback are not proven

verification.method: Confirm paid-project backup and PITR settings, rehearse restore on an isolated branch, and approve the no-split-brain rollback plan before new target writes begin.
successCriteria: A named restore point, retention policy, and restore owner exist for the target project.
Migration and Edge Function rollback instructions identify the prior artifact and validation command.
After target writes begin, rollback requires reconciliation rather than blindly pointing traffic back to the source.
evidenceRequirements: Redacted backup and PITR configuration attestation.
Redacted isolated restore rehearsal and rollback decision record.

blockers.title: Production error capture, security monitoring, and owner alerting are not proven

verification.method: Configure Vercel and Supabase log retention, error capture, release tagging, health checks, alert routes, and a synthetic error drill without sending customer data to logs.
successCriteria: Client, Edge Function, database, Auth, and deployment failures have an observable route and release correlation.
Alerts reach a named operational role for elevated authentication failures, public abuse blocks, failed notifications, job failures, and data errors.
Log redaction prevents credentials, booking tokens, customer contact details, and raw request bodies from becoming observability data.
evidenceRequirements: Redacted synthetic error drill and alert receipt record.
Redacted observability configuration inventory.

blockers.title: SGT business-date behavior is not verified across browser, database, reporting, and scheduled jobs

verification.method: Run synthetic booking, sale, report, retention, and scheduled-job cases immediately before and after SGT midnight from SGT and non-SGT browser time zones.
successCriteria: A Singapore business day renders, stores, reports, and schedules consistently at boundary times.
Browser-local time does not move a booking, report bucket, commission period, retention window, or reminder into the wrong SGT day.
The operating model documents the current Singapore-only timezone decision and the path to per-business timezones before regional expansion.
evidenceRequirements: Redacted two-timezone boundary test report.
Redacted job-schedule verification using SGT timestamps only.

blockers.title: The deployed application is not proven to use only the Singapore target URL, key, and CSP origin set

verification.method: Deploy the approved release, inspect served HTML and headers, then run an authenticated browser smoke test that records the target project reference without recording credentials.
successCriteria: The app contains one approved target project reference and no source-project endpoint.
CSP connect sources match the target and required CDN origins only.
The canonical production URL, redirect settings, and frontend build all reach the same target project.
evidenceRequirements: Redacted served-header and build-reference check.
Redacted owner and staff browser smoke result.

blockers.title: Test accounts, known credentials, API keys, and migration access have not been removed or rotated

verification.method: Inventory production identities and secrets, remove test users and fixtures, rotate any credential disclosed during development or migration, and verify least-privilege access afterwards.
successCriteria: No documented test login, shared password, development API key, source database credential, or migration credential remains valid for production access.
Operator and service access use named roles with least privilege and recovery owners.
Synthetic smoke accounts are isolated, labeled, and excluded from merchant reporting or removed after verification.
evidenceRequirements: Redacted credential-rotation and access-review attestation.
Redacted test-account removal or isolation verification.

blockers.title: End-to-end SME workflows and the first production monitoring window are not verified

verification.method: Run the final smoke matrix after URL and key cutover, retain the source read-only, then review the first production monitoring window before declaring the release stable.
successCriteria: Owner, staff, customer join, booking, booking management, sale, loyalty, credit, referral, reporting, export, and authorization-denial workflows pass with synthetic data.
No unexplained Vercel, Edge Function, Auth, database, or notification error trend appears during the monitoring window.
Rollback readiness remains intact and the source is not writable after traffic has cut over.
evidenceRequirements: Redacted end-to-end smoke matrix.
Redacted monitoring-window review and rollback-readiness confirmation.

blockers.title: The static deployment artifact and its public routes are not proven to build together

verification.method: Run the repository build and inspect the deployed static artifact after all required public pages, policy pages, and gateway routes are included in the intended release surface.
successCriteria: The production build passes without excluding a required public page or including an unintended static artifact.
Privacy, terms, and data-request links resolve from the public join, portal, authentication, and direct-route entry points.
The deployed artifact matches the reviewed route allowlist and security headers.
evidenceRequirements: Redacted successful build and route-manifest result.
Redacted deployed public-route smoke report.
